source: Harder rebuild: 24 borderline selfaware items (hardest answerable/unanswerable distinctions), 12 authored NONE/unanswerable cases (questions with subtle missing context that look answerable but aren't), 10 authored close-distractor items (questions that look unanswerable but have factual answers — and vice versa), 10 socratic-clarify items, 8 cynefin-classify items. Replaces the original selfaware-heavy pool whose trivia-style answerability questions were too easy for claude-sonnet-4-6.
leakage:
Selfaware dataset (OkayestProgrammer/selfAware) contains general-knowledge questions with answerability labels — only the most borderline 24 of 70 items were selected. Leakage risk is moderate for common trivia facts, low for obscure ones. Authored NONE cases and close-distractor items are freshly authored with ZERO external leakage. Socratic-clarify and cynefin-classify datasets are locally authored — zero leakage risk. GLM distractor items may have training data overlap.

Source breakdown: 1 external dataset (selfaware, borderline subset) with moderate training-data leakage; 3 authored datasets (NONE cases, close-distractors, socratic-clarify, cynefin-classify) with zero external leakage.

label.definition: Abstention/answerability: whether the model correctly determines if a question is answerable with verifiable information, or correctly decides whether to ask a clarifying question. Close distractors are designed to look unanswerable but have specific factual answers (testing false negatives) and vice versa (testing false positives).

calibration.improvement_notes: Rebuilt pool with 12 authored NONE cases and 10 close-distractor items, plus only the hardest borderline selfaware items. Baseline dropped from 88% to 84% — a 4pp improvement, but still above the [0.40,0.70] calibration band. The solver remains too capable at answerability classification even on harder items.
calibration.ceiling_needs_harder_data: CEILING-NEEDS-HARDER-DATA (Invariant #4, not a kill). After a good-faith effort sourcing 12 NONE cases with subtle missing context and 10 close distractors in both directions, and selecting only the hardest 24/70 selfaware items, claude-sonnet-4-6 still achieves 84% placebo baseline on this family at n=25, K_TRIALS=5. The answerability/abstention classification task is intrinsically well-suited to this solver's capabilities — it reliably distinguishes answerable from unanswerable questions even when the distinction is subtle. Further item hardening (e.g., multi-hop dependency questions, adversarial ambiguity exploiting solver priors, questions requiring real-time verification) may eventually bring the baseline into band, but the current good-faith effort improved only from 88% to 84%. This is an honest ceiling — NOT a kill verdict. Recommend: either accept the abstention surface as an intentional ceiling-tolerant measurement (comparing solver-with-skill vs solver-without-skill at the ceiling, where any lift is hard evidence of genuine skill value), or source items that exploit known solver weaknesses in answerability judgment.

item_class_definitions.target: Borderline answerability questions: selfaware borderline items, NONE/unanswerable cases with subtle missing context, socratic-clarify items requiring judgment about when to ask clarifying questions, and cynefin-classify items about ordered vs unordered problems.
item_class_definitions.near_miss: This family intentionally has 0 near-miss items. The decisive split rule requires >=1 target + >=1 distractor, which is satisfied.
item_class_definitions.distractor: Authored close-distractor items: questions that look unanswerable but have specific factual answers (testing false negatives — solver wrongly says 'unanswerable') and vice versa. The solver should NOT trivially reject these as unanswerable.

pool_limitations.near_miss_zero: This family intentionally has 0 near-miss items. The decisive split rule requires >=1 target + >=1 distractor, which is satisfied (54 targets, 10 distractors). This is a known pool limitation — proceed without near-miss items.
